scope_boundary: On two finite authored partially observable environments, five seeds, ten matched arms, 11,250 total episodes, and 6,000 held-out episodes, the frozen governed count-table path passed every integrity and baseline gate plus six aggregate directional ablation signatures, with exact governed replacement and rollback in 10/10 environment-seed runs.
transition_reason: All preregistered integrity and both-environment baseline gates passed, all six aggregate ablation directions passed, reference leakage was zero, and exact identity, replay, authority, replacement, rollback, and consolidation-lineage checks survived independent validation and ten laundering mutations.
required_artifacts: neural-world-model implementation and matched neural baselines
independently implemented simulator and external evaluator
reliable shift and causal intervention evidence
deployment-like authority, effect, recovery, and total-cost evidence

evidence_quality_dimension_deltas.reproducibility: exact hashes, deterministic finite simulators, five seeds, complete traces, and validator recomputation
evidence_quality_dimension_deltas.adversarial_strength: ten arms, six causal ablations, shifted heldout halves, and ten laundering mutations
evidence_quality_dimension_deltas.validity: independent closed-trace scorer and exact replay checks; simulators and evaluator remain internally authored
evidence_quality_dimension_deltas.transfer_distance: one meaningfully different authored domain with a declared shared state interface; no natural or open-world transfer

negative_results: governed task success was 0.02333334 and 0.03000000 below the ungoverned predictive arm
active information reduced workshop accuracy by 0.00666667 while improving service-mesh accuracy by 0.03666667
the environments were finite authored tables and no neural world model was trained
downgrade_triggers: design, code, environment, raw, result, or schema digest mismatch
reference-state leakage or unmatched hidden trajectories
outcome-aware repair or undisclosed retry discovery
failure of exact metric recomputation, replacement, rollback, or lineage
finite synthetic scope described as neural, open-world, deployment, safety, transfer, or SOTA evidence
chapter-core promotion inferred from this non-core transition
promotion_burden: Replicate with independently implemented environments and evaluators, neural and non-neural current baselines, natural and open-world tasks, stronger shift and intervention tests, complete total-cost accounting, deployment-like bounded effects, and heterogeneous transfer before any broader world-model, planning, memory, architecture, safety, or RSI claim.
limitations: Bayesian count-table policies rather than learned neural world models
shared three-state abstraction declared in advance
weak shift detection and modest intervention-effect prediction
no natural task, physical effect, production service, or external assessment
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; no external human review requested or claimed
non_claims: does not promote any chapter core claim above argument
does not establish a neural world model, learned latent representation, language-model improvement, or human developmental process
does not establish open-world object permanence, general causal understanding, reliable anomaly detection, or transfer beyond the two authored environments
does not establish simulator adequacy, sim-to-real transfer, deployment safety, security, privacy, autonomous authority, SOTA, AGI, or ASI
does not authorize publication, release, or a new chapter
